principles: Use 3D component placement as a spatial guide, not as literal final geometry.
Preserve the original perspective image composition, camera angle, room scale, and main materials unless the selected strategy requires a visible change.
Show only the selected strategy or selected strategy bundle, not every possible intervention.
Keep the retrofit intervention attached to the correct wall, window, floor, or ceiling zone.
Do not show diagnostic labels, colored masks, bounding boxes, or procedural support placeholders in the final perspective image.
If an intervention is exterior but the image is interior-facing, show its visible indoor consequence where plausible: shade line, tinted glazing, filtered daylight, or edge of awning/louver.

strategy_visual_translation.external_shading_layer: subtle exterior shading device aligned with the selected opening; filtered daylight on interior surfaces
strategy_visual_translation.solar_control_glazing_layer: slightly cooler/tinted glass appearance while retaining transparency and original frame geometry
strategy_visual_translation.interior_window_covering_layer: natural fabric/blind/shade mounted on the interior opening head with correct scale and clearance
strategy_visual_translation.ceiling_air_movement_layer: ceiling fan centered in the occupied zone, sized to the room and clear of curtains
strategy_visual_translation.wall_inner_lining_layer: thin interior wall finish layer, visually subtle and not protruding outside the facade
strategy_visual_translation.biophilic_floor_cluster: plant cluster in daylight corner or beside opening without blocking circulation
strategy_visual_translation.biophilic_wall_adjacent_layer: wall-adjacent planting support beside the opening, attached to interior wall face
strategy_visual_translation.hanging_lightweight_greenery: small hanging plants from a safe rail/bracket, not blocking the active opening

negative_prompt_rules: no plants blocking sliding door operation
no unrealistic structural facade change unless selected and permitted
